source_jobs.title: Senior Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Medely is a high-growth series-C technology start-up reimagining the future of healthcare work by providing an on-demand marketplace and workforce management tools. With the support of top-tier investors, Medely has quickly become a go-to platform for healthcare professionals to instantly access high-paying jobs with the freedom and flexibility to work when and where they want. 



As a fully remote team of sharp, collaborative, and entrepreneurial individuals, we are dedicated to redefining the way healthcare workers and facilities work together to provide for patients. We are looking for passionate and skilled professionals to join our team and help us take on tough challenges in this fast-expanding healthcare industry segment.  



Join us in our mission to transform the healthcare staffing industry and improve patient lives!



The Role

We’re looking for a seasoned Data Scientist to help solve high-impact business problems across Medely’s rapidly growing healthcare marketplace. As a senior member of the Data team, you’ll shape product strategy, lead large-scale initiatives end-to-end, and partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Operations to build science-driven solutions that improve marketplace performance.



This is a highly strategic role for someone who thrives in ambiguity, loves solving complex problems, and wants to directly influence how a category-defining marketplace evolves.



How You Will Make an Impact

 - Identify meaningful business and product opportunities that are currently unaddressed, and develop the insights, models, and systems needed to drive measurable impact

 - Build advanced machine learning, optimization, and analytics solutions that improve marketplace efficiency at scale

 - Design and execute experiments and observational studies that generate actionable business and product insights

 - Partner cross-functionally to define key metrics, improve logging and instrumentation, and build data visualizations and diagnostic tools that enable smarter decision-making

 - Deliver ad hoc analyses and investigate performance trends, operational issues, and metrics-based opportunities with minimal guidance

 - Translate complex analyses into clear recommendations and compelling narratives that influence strategic direction across the organization

 - Help orient teams around data-driven product development and foster a culture of thoughtful experimentation and rigorous decision-making



Who You Are

 - Ph.D. or M.S. in a quantitative field with 3+ years of experience, or a Bachelor’s degree with 7+ years of experience in Data Science, Analytics, or a related discipline

 - Strong foundation in experimental design and analysis, with the ability to translate findings into meaningful business or product decisions

 - Advanced proficiency in SQL and experience leveraging business intelligence tools to uncover insights and guide strategy

 - Competency in Python, R, or another scripting language for data analysis, modeling, and problem-solving

 - Ability to distill complex data into clear, compelling narratives that influence decision-making

 - Proven success partnering cross-functionally and communicating effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders alike



What Sets You Apart

 - Experience applying data science to marketplace optimization challenges, including pricing, matching, or supply-demand dynamics

 - A builder’s mindset - you thrive in fast-moving environments, embrace ambiguity, and take ownership from insight through execution

 - Naturally curious and eager to explore new tools, methods, and approaches that unlock better outcomes

 - Passionate about using data to shape products that create meaningful value for customers and the business



Why Medely: Benefits & Perks

 - Competitive Compensation: Based on experience and performance

 - Long-term Incentives: 401k

 - Healthcare Benefits: Full suite of benefits including medical, dental, and vision insuran

source_jobs.title: Staff Front End Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Medely is a high-growth series-C technology start-up reimagining the future of healthcare work by providing an on-demand marketplace and workforce management tools. With the support of top-tier investors, Medely has quickly become a go-to platform for healthcare professionals to instantly access high-paying jobs with the freedom and flexibility to work when and where they want. 



As a fully remote team of sharp, collaborative, and entrepreneurial individuals, we are dedicated to redefining the way healthcare workers and facilities work together to provide for patients. We are looking for passionate and skilled professionals to join our team and help us take on tough challenges in this fast-expanding healthcare industry segment.  



Join us in our mission to transform the healthcare staffing industry and improve patient lives!



The Role

At Medely, we’re transforming how healthcare professionals and facilities connect: faster, smarter, and at scale. Every experience we build helps nurses, clinicians, and hospitals focus less on logistics and more on care.



As our Staff Front End Engineer, you’ll lead the front end vision for one of our core product teams. You’ll partner with backend and mobile engineers to design elegant, performant, and scalable user experiences that power both sides of our marketplace. From architecture decisions to mentoring engineers, you’ll shape how healthcare staffing feels and functions in the modern era.



The Impact You'll Make:

 - Act as the front end technical lead for cross-functional, agile teams building high-impact web applications.

 - Drive architecture decisions and code standards to ensure scalability, maintainability, and performance.

 - Collaborate with product, design, and backend partners to translate ideas into delightful, accessible, and performant interfaces.

 - Lead by example - write high-quality, testable code and perform thoughtful code reviews.

 - Champion modern front end technologies, developer tools, and patterns to continuously evolve our stack.

 - Mentor and elevate other engineers through knowledge sharing and collaboration.

Who You Are:

 - 7+ years of professional front end development experience, with deep expertise of 3+ years in React (including state management, animations, and Webpack).

 - Strong understanding of JavaScript/TypeScript and modern frameworks; React Native experience is a plus.

 - Proven experience leading or architecting complex, interactive applications end-to-end.

 - Passion for design systems, performance optimization, and crafting exceptional user experiences.

 - Track record of mentoring engineers and improving team culture and process.

 - Collaborative mindset - you’re equally comfortable whiteboarding solutions or rolling up your sleeves to ship them.

 - Awareness of scalability and performance considerations across browsers and devices.

 - BS/BA in Computer Science or related technical field (or equivalent practical experience).

What Will Set You Apart

 - You think beyond the code. You care about how things are built and why they matter to the user.

 - You’ve owned complex, high-traffic web applications and made measurable improvements to performance, scalability, or developer experience.

 - You have strong opinions, loosely held: you can articulate architectural decisions clearly but stay open to new ideas.

 - You find joy in details: accessibility, animation timing, responsiveness - the subtle things that turn a good UI into a great one.



Why Medely: Benefits & Perks

 - Competitive Compensation: Based on experience and performance

 - Long-term Incentives: 401k

 - Healthcare Benefits: Full suite of benefits including medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - Flexibility: We believe that work/life balance is important, so we offer flexible and unlimited PTO

 - Paid parental leave

 - Purpose: Join a growing mission-oriented startup that is modernizing the healthcare industry nationally!

 - Ownership

source_jobs.title: Manager, Business Intelligence
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Medely is a high-growth series-C technology start-up reimagining the future of healthcare work by providing an on-demand marketplace and workforce management tools. With the support of top-tier investors, Medely has quickly become a go-to platform for healthcare professionals to instantly access high-paying jobs with the freedom and flexibility to work when and where they want. 



As a fully remote team of sharp, collaborative, and entrepreneurial individuals, we are dedicated to redefining the way healthcare workers and facilities work together to provide for patients. We are looking for passionate and skilled professionals to join our team and help us take on tough challenges in this fast-expanding healthcare industry segment.  



Join us in our mission to transform the healthcare staffing industry and improve patient lives!



The Opportunity

Full-Time · Reports to VP, Financial Operations

Medely is building the infrastructure layer for healthcare staffing - a marketplace that connects thousands of healthcare professionals with facilities that need them, in real time. It's a complex, data-rich business, and the decisions that determine how we grow, where we invest, and how we serve our customers are increasingly being driven by the quality of our analytics.



That's where you come in.



We're looking for a Manager of Business Intelligence who is equal parts analytical rigor and business instinct - someone who doesn't just build dashboards, but builds understanding. You'll lead a BI Analyst, own the execution of our BI roadmap, and work directly with the VP of Financial Operations and cross-functional leadership to turn data into decisions that move the business.



If you're the kind of person who finds satisfaction in making a complex system legible - who gets energized by ambiguous problems, clean data models, and stakeholders who actually use what you build - this role was designed for you.



What You'll Own

 - Be hands-on with the work. You won't just delegate. You'll write SQL, build dashboards, run analytical deep-dives, and produce models that generate real product and business insights. You're still a craftsperson.

 - Drive self-service analytics across the org. A big part of this role is making data accessible to people who aren't analysts. You'll train and enable business partners to navigate Delphina (our BI platform), set the governance standards that keep data trustworthy, and build the infrastructure so teams can answer their own questions -without creating chaos.

 - Translate complexity into clarity. You'll partner with operational and functional stakeholders to monitor business health, surface trends, and bring structure to ambiguous problems. The best outcome isn't a beautiful chart - it's a decision that wouldn't have been possible without you.

 - Build for scale, not just today. You'll contribute to a BI roadmap that balances short-term execution with long-term architecture - including alerting, anomaly detection, and applying AI/ML techniques to accelerate insight generation where it creates real leverage.

 - Work directly with leadership. Reporting into the VP of Financial Operations means high visibility and real exposure. Your work will directly inform how Medely's leadership team thinks about growth, operations, and strategy.



Who You Are:

 - A minimum of 5 years in analytics, business intelligence, data science, or a related field - with at least one year managing a direct report.

 - Advanced SQL is non-negotiable. You write clean, optimized queries and you understand the systems underneath them.

 - Strong proficiency in a BI tool (Tableau, Power BI, or similar) and a real understanding of what makes a dashboard useful vs. just pretty.

 - Python or another scripting language for data manipulation and workflow automation.

 - Experience structuring ambiguous problems and communicating findings to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Comfort with experimentatio

source_jobs.title: Account Executive (West Coast)
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Medely is a high-growth series-C technology start-up reimagining the future of healthcare work by providing an on-demand marketplace and workforce management tools. With the support of top-tier investors, Medely has quickly become a go-to platform for healthcare professionals to instantly access high-paying jobs with the freedom and flexibility to work when and where they want. 



As a fully remote team of sharp, collaborative, and entrepreneurial individuals, we are dedicated to redefining the way healthcare workers and facilities work together to provide for patients. We are looking for passionate and skilled professionals to join our team and help us take on tough challenges in this fast-expanding healthcare industry segment.  



Join us in our mission to transform the healthcare staffing industry and improve patient lives!



Location: Remote (Must be in PST or MST)

Compensation: $124,000 - $138,000 OTE (base + commission at plan). Commission is uncapped with accelerators, so earnings increase significantly when you exceed quota.




THE ROLE:

As our Account Executive at Medely, you’ll own the full sales cycle with outpatient healthcare facilities: from prospecting and discovery through tailored solution demos and closing. While your focus will be on the SMB market today, you’ll be working in a consultative, multi-stakeholder sales environment designed to set you up for success as Medely expands into more complex, mid-market opportunities.



You’ll combine strategic territory planning with high-quality, consistent activity to drive adoption of Medely’s healthcare staffing platform. Success in this role comes from understanding each facility’s operational and staffing challenges and positioning Medely as a long-term, value-driven partner.




THE IMPACT YOU’LL MAKE:


WITHIN ONE MONTH, YOU WILL…

 - Ramp quickly on Medely’s platform, value proposition, and the healthcare staffing landscape.

 - Begin 1:1s with your manager, align on your 30-60-90 plan, and shadow discovery and demo calls.
   Learn the sales tech stack (Salesforce, Outreach, Gong, Google Workspace) and territory structure.

 - Start building your outbound prospect list and following up on inbound leads.


WITHIN THREE MONTHS, YOU WILL…

 - Own a territory plan for outpatient healthcare facilities in your assigned region.

 - Run consultative discovery calls that uncover operational pain points and staffing needs.

 - Demonstrate Medely’s platform with confidence, tailoring use cases to each facility type.

 - Generate pipeline primarily through self-prospecting; while some inbound opportunities will come your way, the majority of your success will come from your own outreach and relationship-building.

 - Begin closing your first deals.


WITHIN SIX MONTHS, YOU WILL…

 - Consistently meet or exceed activity metrics and pipeline targets.
   Navigate multi-stakeholder sales cycles, engaging both clinical and administrative decision-makers.

 - Build a steady stream of net new opportunities while also expanding relationships with existing customers.
   Partner with Customer Success to ensure smooth onboarding and high adoption.


WITHIN ONE YEAR, YOU WILL…

 - Be a top-performing AE, exceeding quota and contributing to Medely’s growth in your market.

 - Share market insights with leadership to refine our go-to-market approach.

 - Mentor newer AEs as they ramp.


WHO YOU ARE:

 - A minimum of 4 years of full-cycle, quota-carrying sales experience in software/technology; healthcare or regulated industry experience a plus.

 - Skilled in consultative, solution-based selling with a track record of closing multi-contact opportunities.

 - Demonstrated ability to generate pipeline through self-prospecting.

 - Comfortable engaging with multiple decision-makers (clinical, operational, executive) within an account.

 - Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and resilient in a remote sales environment.

 - BS/BA degree or equivalent in relevant sales experie

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Medely is a high-growth series-C technology start-up reimagining the future of healthcare work by providing an on-demand marketplace and workforce management tools. With the support of top-tier investors, Medely has quickly become a go-to platform for healthcare professionals to instantly access high-paying jobs with the freedom and flexibility to work when and where they want. 



As a fully remote team of sharp, collaborative, and entrepreneurial individuals, we are dedicated to redefining the way healthcare workers and facilities work together to provide for patients. We are looking for passionate and skilled professionals to join our team and help us take on tough challenges in this fast-expanding healthcare industry segment.  



Join us in our mission to transform the healthcare staffing industry and improve patient lives!



Own the Marketplace Powering Healthcare Work

Healthcare facilities need a faster, smarter, and more reliable way to connect with the clinical professionals who keep care moving.



That’s what Medely is building.



Our Flex marketplace is the core of our business today, connecting healthcare facilities with qualified professionals across the country. It’s proven, scaled, and business-critical. Now we’re focused on what comes next: improving how facilities engage, how professionals find the right opportunities, how matching works, and how we drive deeper retention and expansion.



We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager who wants real ownership of those problems.



This is not a “take tickets and ship features” PM role. You’ll work across complex marketplace dynamics - supply and demand, matching, trust, conversion, and retention - and be expected to challenge assumptions, define the right problems, and make thoughtful tradeoffs that drive measurable outcomes.



Why Now?

You’ll join at a point where the marketplace has real traction, real data, and real business impact - with meaningful room to shape what comes next.



You’ll have direct access to leadership, strong cross-functional partners, and ownership of product decisions that affect both the healthcare facilities we serve and the professionals who power them.



What You'll Own

 - Define the vision, strategy, and roadmap for key areas of the Flex marketplace

 - Own major product initiatives from discovery through launch and iteration, including high-quality PRDs, clear success criteria, and cross-functional alignment

 - Use product data, marketplace signals, customer insights, and user research to identify the highest-value problems to solve

 - Make strategic tradeoffs across conversion, retention, supply and demand, matching quality, operational efficiency, and user experience

 - Partner deeply with Engineering and Design while driving alignment across Product Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, Clinical, Finance, and leadership

 - Measure what ships, learn from outcomes, and feed those insights back into product strategy

 - Identify risks, gaps, and opportunities before they become obvious - and bring a point of view on what we should do next

 - Apply AI and LLMs pragmatically as a force multiplier - both to simplify user workflows and to supercharge your own team's operational velocity



Who You Are:

You want ownership, not hand-holding.



You’re comfortable turning complex systems, messy user behavior, and business-critical tradeoffs into intuitive, elegant user experiences. You can zoom out to understand marketplace dynamics and zoom in to define the workflow, metric, requirement, or edge case that determines whether something succeeds.



You’re analytical, highly accountable, and willing to challenge the direction when the evidence points somewhere better. You don’t just move work forward - you make the work sharper.



You’ll likely be successful here if you have:

 - 5+ years of product management experience shipping software in startup, marketplace, SaaS, or scaled technology environments

 - Experien

notes: High-growth series-C tech startup with a strong focus on engineering and product roles.
